title: Asuka vs Shinji Fist Fight Who Wins
display_title: Asuka vs Shinji Fist Fight Who Wins
wikitext:
[]: Asuka vs. Shinji Fist Fight: Who Wins?             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Sun May 27, 2012 12:07 am Asuka seems like the obvious answer, but I'm not so sure. Shinji's reluctance to pilot isn't as much about cowardice as some think -- he's legitimately frightened by his destructive power. His own Berserker Mode is always lurking beneath the surface. Check out his face during the Zeruel fight, he's delighted to be tearing this fucker apart. I say Asuka wins if she ends things quick. On the other hand, if she prolongs the torture to humiliate him, Shinji's rage rises enough to make him competitive. So which Shinji shows up? The guilt-fetishist Toji smacked down? Or the angry child that will snap a neck? Place your bets!

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sun May 27, 2012 1:52 am ...

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun May 27, 2012 3:04 am Shinji would never even raise a fist against Asuka; it's simply not in his nature to get into conflict with others, especially not a physical conflict. I picture him taking the beating the entire time; that would be whenever Asuka vents out all her frustration, or when she realizes that she's failing to provoke him.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Sun May 27, 2012 5:14 am Shinji by choke out.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Sun May 27, 2012 8:42 am he's legitimately frightened by his destructive power.

[]:             Quote Post by Ceimoa Nan » Sun May 27, 2012 9:20 am Sachi wrote: Shinji would never even raise a fist against Asuka; it's simply not in his nature to get into conflict with others, especially not a physical conflict.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun May 27, 2012 10:33 am There's a doujin with almost the same title as this thread. This is how it ends -- which is much closer to how I would have expected the beach scene to have played out with Asuka as she was when first she came to NERV.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun May 27, 2012 10:50 am You people are silly. If they ever seriously fought Asuka would kick his ass so hard that Gendo might actually be concerned. And then Rei would drop by one evening and Asuka would go missing.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun May 27, 2012 11:12 am NemZ wrote: Asuka would kick his ass so hard

[]:             Quote Post by Archont » Sun May 27, 2012 12:43 pm Shinji has no motivation to fist fight, especially with girl. But if he start fight back, Asuka would be so startled she would let him win. Just like in Instrumentality. But it's highly unlikely that he would fight back.

[]:             Quote Post by driftking18594 » Sun May 27, 2012 12:51 pm Asuka would win, and whip his ass.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun May 27, 2012 3:18 pm They wouldn't fight. Asuka's aggression is grossly overhyped (outside of the action arc, which was full of slapstick comedy, her only instance of striking someone was the slap she gave Shinji when he confronted her about Kaji's death) and Shinji had to literally have his mind broken several times over before he could even contemplate the thought of going physical against someone else. As for hidden rage . . . Asuka doesn't have it, and in Shinji's case it's just fight-or-flight in action. Unless he has some actual reason to fight (and he'll never have a reason to fight Asuka) he'll just sit there and take it because he just doesn't care. If some plot contrivance manipulated them into fighting, and somehow got Shinji to make an effort rather than just being the whipped little boy he is, Asuka would probably wipe the floor with him. Even without formal combat training her performance in EoE demonstrates a basic level of tactical awareness that Shinji just doesn't have. She'd also be a lot more willing to do it if she had to, since she lacks Shinji's over-developed sense of empathy. But as I said it wouldn't happen, since the kids are who they are.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun May 27, 2012 6:37 pm Ceimoa Nan wrote: He threatened to take out a chunk of Nerv HQ, and I see no reason to doubt him. But I would agree it'd be difficult for him to initiate conflict with Asuka of all people. She'd have to do something really, really... Wait a second. Shinji tried to straight-up murder Asuka, with his bare hands. Twice, if you count what happened in pre-Instrumentality.

[]:             Quote Post by Aska89 » Sun May 27, 2012 7:13 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: Misato sends them to their rooms without dinner. Pen-Pen wins.

[]: Re: Asuka vs. Shinji Fist Fight: Who Wins?             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Sun May 27, 2012 7:33 pm Shinji is a gentleman. Asuka would take advantage of that. Short of the choking scenario, Asuka wins. :P

[]:             Quote Post by Cody MacArthur Fett » Sun May 27, 2012 8:28 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Shinji has a slight advantage in height and presumably weight , and presumably in testosterone, Asuka has whatever training she has received. Both have the capability to become enraged -- except when they do come to blows, in both cases, Asuka is beaten psychologically at the outset.

[]:             Quote Post by CX1329 » Sun May 27, 2012 9:04 pm Asuka wins at 01:48 into the first round, via kimura.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun May 27, 2012 9:35 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: Misato sends them to their rooms without dinner. Pen-Pen wins.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Sun May 27, 2012 9:59 pm Bagheera wrote: Asuka's about to give him the beating of a lifetime, when it dawns on her that he's utterly at her mercy. She chooses discretion over valor, takes him to her room, and has her way with him. Shinji, being the obedient lad that he is, takes it like a man. Everybody wins!

[]:             Quote Post by Cody MacArthur Fett » Sun May 27, 2012 10:09 pm Stryker wrote: Shinji got his ass handed to him. How is that a "Everyone Wins" situation, even if Shinji took it like a man.? Unless your suggesting a sexual confrontation, which I would find it strange that you would suggest that, of all people.
